page: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA T4G GPU from the Turing family with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48574.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11551.0. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 952.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.17 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 1892.97 GB/sec, though the Passmark memory score is categorized as weak at 1813.51. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves an average text generation speed of 15.24 tokens/sec on a medium 7B LLM, but exhibits poor performance on text generation for smaller 135M models at 42.38 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5g.8xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile that pairs energy-efficient ARM architecture with entry-level GPU acceleration, making it a viable option for workloads requiring GPU resources without the high cost of enterprise-grade accelerators. This instance is suited for Android game streaming, graphics rendering, machine learning inference on medium-sized models, and general-purpose GPU-accelerated applications that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based, GPU-accelerated server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance relies on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, though it lacks local storage. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.86, alongside weak single-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small blocks but weak under Passmark testing. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for entry-level GPU workloads, making it suitable for graphics rendering, Android game streaming, and medium-model machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU. It is designed for cost-effective graphics rendering and machine learning inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 32 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 16 GB NVIDIA T4G GPU for graphics workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based server combining dedicated Graviton processors with NVIDIA GPU acceleration for cost-effective graphics and machine learning inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores.
Equipped with a single NVIDIA T4G GPU featuring 16 GB of VRAM.
Includes 64.0 GB of system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core benchmarks.
No local storage is bundled with this instance type.
Best suited for graphics rendering, Android game streaming, and medium-model ML inference.
